channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: The Latest: Trump delays more tariffs amid economic fears
item.description: Wall Street is having another tough day amid anxiety over President Donald Trump’s often-changing tariff proposals.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: How Trump justifies his tariffs
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — To President Donald Trump, “tariff” is more than “the most beautiful word in the dictionary,” something he says often. Tariffs, in Trump’s view, are also a cure for a number of the nation’s ills and the tool to reach new heights.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Price fixing on essential goods in Nevada targeted, but businesses are not on board
item.description: A bill that targets fraudulent and deceptive actions that try to manipulate prices of essential goods and services prompted a backlash from the business community on Wednesday in Carson City.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florida House speaker responds to Democrats call to investigate insurance companies
item.description: Democrats at the Florida State House are calling on Gov. Ron DeSantis and the House Speaker to further investigate Florida's insurance companies.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Use of military aircraft for deportations has stopped, source says
item.description: A Defense Department source says the Pentagon has not received a request for military aircraft since Saturday, March 1.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Republicans need to cut Medicaid to hit budget savings target, CBO finds
item.description: Republicans can’t achieve their goal of slashing $2 trillion in federal spending over the next decade without cutting Medicaid, according to the nonpartisan Congressional Budget Office (CBO).
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Vance suggests other industries won't get tariff carve-outs
item.description: Vice President Vance on Wednesday said that industries other than U.S. automakers have asked for exemptions on tariffs, but suggested that the Trump administration would not extend further carve outs.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Trump grants one-month exemption for US automakers from new tariffs on imports from Mexico, Canada
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is granting a one-month exemption on his stiff new tariffs on imports from Mexico and Canada for U.S. automakers, as worries persist that the newly launched trade war could crush domestic manufacturing.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: State of Democra-Z: Three young voters weigh in on Trump’s first weeks in office
item.description: After President Trump’s address to Congress Tuesday night, MPR News host Nina Moini checks in with three college-age voters with differing political views.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini and Alanna Elder, MPR News

item.title: Nevada Democrats react to Trump's speech; state Republicans applaud his vision
item.description: Democrats who represent Nevada in Washington, D.C., condemned what they see as a failure by President Donald Trump to lower prices for Nevada families, while state Republicans applauded his leadership the morning after his address to a joint session of Congress Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Republican introduces censure resolution against Al Green
item.description: The House will vote this week on a resolution from Rep. Dan Newhouse (R-Wash.) to censure Rep. Al Green (D-Texas) for his outburst at President Trump’s speech to a joint session of Congress on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Veterans are speaking out on the Trump administration’s plans to cut the VA’s budget
item.description: A handful of veterans speaking to The Associated Press are divided over the Trump administration’s proposed cuts to the Department of Veterans Affairs. Stephen Watson is a former Marine who lives in Jesup, Georgia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Businesses scramble to contain fallout from Trump’s tariffs on Canada, China and Mexico
item.description: A Minnesota farmer worries about the price of fertilizer. A San Diego entrepreneur deals with an unexpected increase in the cost of remodeling a restaurant. A Midwestern sheet metal fabricator bemoans the prospect of higher aluminum prices.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Gov. DeSantis endorses permanent protections from vaccines mandates in Florida
item.description: Gov. Ron DeSantis is set to speak in Tampa with his wife and the Florida surgeon general on Wednesday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Katlyn Fernandez, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What Trump’s aid freeze means for Ukraine war
item.description: President Donald Trump’s freeze on U.S. aid going to Ukraine threatens to create severe problems for Kyiv in its war against Russia, leaving the country more vulnerable to aerial attacks on population centers and key infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, Fox 5

item.title: Democrats disrupt, protest and wear pained expressions for Trump’s speech
item.description: House Democrats greeted President Trump’s return to Congress on Tuesday with a spirited series of disruptive protests — and a number of painful looks as they suffered through an address in which the returning president repeatedly cajoled and mocked them.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: DeSantis' State of the State: government accountability a key focus
item.description: Gov. Ron DeSantis gave his state of the state address Tuesday and offered his plan for the year ahead. Members of the legislature, and leaders past and present gathered in Tallahassee for the speech Tuesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nevada 'a perfect storm for homicides' in domestic violence cases, lawmakers told
item.description: FBI statistics show Nevada is "a perfect storm for homicides" and the state ranks third for men murdering women, the leader of SafeNest said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Emboldened Trump praises his 'swift action' in address to Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump will stand before a joint session of Congress on Tuesday to give an accounting of his turbulent first weeks in office as a divided nation struggles to keep pace, with some Americans fearing for the country's future while others are cheering him on.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 5 things to watch as Trump delivers joint address to Congress
item.description: President Trump will head to the Capitol on Tuesday for the first joint address of his second term emboldened by a month of mass federal agency slashing, shocking foreign policy moves and a dizzying array of executive actions that have disrupted Washington and beyond.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Trump administration to downsize Social Security Administration
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Elon Musk, the world's wealthiest person, who is also a senior advisor to President Donald Trump, is now setting his sights in recent comments on the Social Security Administration (SSA). "Social Security is the biggest Ponzi scheme of all time," said Musk.
item.dc:creator: Jay Dow, PIX 11

item.title: Wisconsin audit of Trump win finds not a single voting machine error
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — An audit of the November election won by President Donald Trump in swing-state Wisconsin found that not a single vote was counted incorrectly, altered or missed by tabulating machines.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Breastfeeding, second chances and student trustees: A look at new bills in the Nevada Legislature
item.description: A mother-daughter team from Las Vegas had an important message to deliver Friday in Carson City. Breastfeeding is OK, and more people need to know about it.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News
